avoid errors if the database does not exists

can be created for each table.
constraint is a column or a set o
columns that uniquely identifies each row in a table

Every value in a column or a set of columns be unique.
constraint has more than one column, that group of columns is called a
in a column always satisfies a
Defines a condition that each row must be satisfy.
It cannot reference columns from other tables.
Designates a column or a combination of columns as
in the same table or a different table
Every value in a column cannot be

can be removed if no require
Create table from existing database tables
All data will copy but it will not copy constraints. (Except Null)
Create table an empty table based on the definition of an existing
All data will not copy but it will copy constraints.
show all exist tables in database.
Change data type, data size, default values and constraints.
Do not ADD and MODIFY same column in a statement. (Error)
Do not MODIFY more than one column in a statement. (Error)
The string must put inside a

can be used to insert current date or time.
can be used to insert a null value in a specific cell but we must make sure the column of the specific cell belong allow null value.
is the search condition and only copy the data which satisfy the search condition.

and only update the data which satisfy the search condition.
of the Row We Need To Update
To modify existing values in a table by copy from others.
is the search condition and only update the data which satisfy the search condition.
To delete existing rows from table.
is the search condition and only delete the row of data which satisfy the search condition.
can be used by combining with other code.

is the search condition and only list the row which satisfy the search condition.
SELECT * FROM staff ORDER BY DoorplatNo, Postcode DESC;
Return expr2 if expr1 is NULL.
Return NULL to expr1 if expr1 equal to expr2.
Return the first NOT NULL expr in the list.
